item.title: Disney suffers drop in US theme park visitors as war hits travel
item.description: Growth in revenues from cruises and streaming helps entertainment group mitigate impact

item.title: Apollo asset-backed finance unit swings to loss after MFS hit
item.description: US private capital giant becomes latest financial institution to suffer from exposure to collapsed UK mortgage lender

item.title: The live sellers reinventing sweet shops
item.description: Online storefronts turn vendors into presenters who take orders, package and sell products to global buyers in real time

item.title: UK watchdog launches probe of claims management companies
item.description: FCA to investigate ‘poor practices’ and ‘aggressive marketing’ in sector gaining prominence amid car finance mis-selling scandal

item.title: Kingfisher boss to replace Frans Muller at Dutch retailer Ahold
item.description: Thierry Garnier to step down from DIY group next year to take helm of one of Europe’s largest supermarket chains

item.title: Diageo posts sales boost as buyers load up on drinks ahead of World Cup
item.description: Strong quarterly trading in Latin America helps FTSE 100 group beat forecasts despite US spirits slump

item.title: Novo Nordisk lifts forecasts after strong demand for Wegovy weight-loss pill
item.description: Shares in Danish drugmaker surge on positive signal from results

item.title: Lufthansa flags €1.7bn hit from rising jet fuel prices
item.description: Airline expects to offset costs by raising fares, cutting flights and bringing forward restructuring measures

item.title: Watchdog raises alarm over private credit vulnerabilities
item.description: Warning that banks may find it difficult to manage risks comes a day after HSBC disclosed $400mn hit

item.title: Chinese AI start-up DeepSeek nears $45bn valuation
item.description: Value soars in ongoing fundraising discussions as investors including Tencent seek slice of AI lab

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: Samsung hits $1tn value on AI euphoria
item.description: Memory chip giant’s surge pushes South Korea’s Kospi to new record

item.title: When money went rogue: banking in 19th-century frontier America
item.description: The counterfeit money boom that holds lessons for crypto today

item.title: Kravis and Roberts on KKR at 50
item.description: The original private equity barbarian celebrates its birthday as the $22tn private markets face a fraught moment

item.title: UK supermarkets ask competition watchdog to rein in Aldi and Lidl
item.description: Sainsbury’s, Morrisons and Iceland are seeking to stop discounters preventing them opening stores nearby

item.title: Crypto’s ‘decentralised finance’ sector hit by investor exodus after hacks
item.description: DeFi was once touted as the future of finance but traders have grown concerned over the security of these projects

item.title: Vodafone’s slow-burn strategy is starting to pay off
item.description: The share price has picked up and its valuation as a multiple of forecast earnings has risen to the top of European telcos

item.title: Bezos shakes up Blue Origin staff incentives ahead of SpaceX IPO
item.description: Rocket maker hopes to quell employee uproar after old options expired without a payout

item.title: US shale oil producers are wise to avoid the temptation of higher prices
item.description: With little slack in the system, soaring prices don’t give operators that much of an incentive to let rip

item.title: Santander to remove TSB brand from British high streets after 215 years
item.description: Spanish bank to run combined business as Santander UK after £2.65bn takeover

item.title: Conned by a chatbot
item.description: Like tricksters, LLMs have perfected the art of plausibility

item.title: We must be mindful of the risks of private credit
item.description: The sector remains untested in a severe or prolonged economic downturn

item.title: Fusion start-up backed by Bill Gates plans UK’s first commercial plant
item.description: Most ambitious British timeframe yet for the technology comes as US and China race ahead in funding for sector

item.title: Why the UAE really left Opec
item.description: Leaving the oil cartel is not just a commercial decision — it reflects structural changes in global energy markets

item.title: EU anti-dumping complaints against Chinese chemicals at record high
item.description: A wave of cheap imports is pushing Europe’s industry to ‘breaking point’

item.title: Can Britain’s star tech investor dodge the SaaSpocalypse?
item.description: Delayed IPO of €19bn software group Visma was meant to be a crowning moment for PE group Hg — and London

item.title: The future of Europe’s angry farmers
item.description: The model of political protection and heavy subsidies that has long supported European agriculture might be fracturing

item.title: Start-ups complain they are helping British military modernise on the cheap
item.description: Small tech groups impatient with ‘jam tomorrow’ promise of contracts in return for discounted or free equipment

item.title: Could the US scrap quarterly reporting?
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission has proposed allowing public companies to file earnings reports semi-annually

item.title: AI Labs: Are Anthropic really the good guys?
item.description: Dario Amodei casts his company as the good guys of the AI race. Will that last?
